kept.text:
BitMine Immersion Technologies just added another 101,901 ETH to its stash, bringing total holdings to around 5.08 million ETH.

This is their second major buy in just two weeks, even as they sit on more than $6.5B in unrealized losses. 

Despite the pressure, they’re still staking large amounts of ETH for ongoing yield.

It’s a strong conviction move in the middle of volatility, or a high-risk bet depending on how the market turns.

Either way, big players aren’t stepping back yet. 

Activity like this is already being closely tracked by traders across platforms such as BingX.

kept.text:
Saylor's $STRC has a helluva ponzinomics attached to it, I'll tell ya that much.

I think its Tom Lee's turn to fire one of these bad boys up for $BMNR and ETH. Rumor mill been humming that there's something being worked on behind the scenes that would allow Tom to have more dry powder to accumulate ETH with a similar structure to Stretch.

These two are going to go head to head to eat up as much of these assets supply. While it may not seem super meaningful or impactful today, it will make a difference when the tides shift.

When demand revisits previous levels, there will be less supply available. Simple, really.

profile_bio.description: founder @therollupco

quoted_tweet.text:
Parker White breaks down the actual math behind STRC's 11.5% yield.

"Strategy is forecasting 30% Bitcoin CAGR over a decade, which is below the historical run rate. At 30% growth, paying 11.5% on the preferred is trivial."

"They can sell some Bitcoin if needed. They haven't, because they raise capital from Stretch, the common equity ATM, the converts, and they keep a 2.5-year cash reserve."

"Bitcoin only needs to grow single digits a year for the dividend to pay. Stretch is $8 billion. Bitcoin is $1.6 trillion. The collateralization is massive. The product is structurally over-collateralized."
